program.name: 530A ("Trump") Accounts
program.description_short: Free government-seeded investment account for children under 18
program.description:
530A ("Trump") Accounts are special savings accounts for kids under 18. For children born between January 1, 2025 and December 31, 2028, the government makes a one-time $1,000 deposit into the account. Kids born outside that window can still open an account, they just won't receive the $1,000.

To open an account, a parent or guardian fills out IRS Form 4547. Any family can apply regardless of income, but the child must be a U.S. citizen with a Social Security Number. Only one account is allowed per child.

The money is invested in index funds or ETFs, which are simple investments that follow the stock market (like the S&P 500). The law requires that these investments carry very low fees — no more than 0.10% per year.

A parent or guardian manages the account until the child turns 18. After that, withdrawals are allowed under traditional IRA rules (which say how and when withdrawals can be made).
program.apply_button_description: Learn how to open a Trump Account
program.website_description: Free government-seeded $1,000 investment account for U.S. citizen children born 2025–2028

warning_messages.message: Trump Accounts are not yet open. They are expected to become available on or after July 4, 2026. Check IRS.gov/trumpaccounts for updates.
Your child must have a valid Social Security Number to open a Trump Account. If your child does not yet have an SSN, apply at ssa.gov.

documents.link_text: Apply for a child's SSN at ssa.gov

documents.text: Child's birth certificate or proof of age and citizenship
